Format numbers without the leading zero

Source: R/f0.R
f0.Rd

Format a numeric vector to character, stripping the leading zero from values whose absolute value is less than 1. 0.123 -> ".123", -0.045 -> "-.045", 1.234 -> "1.234".

Used as a vignette / output style helper: funfield's vignettes default to no leading zeros on decimals (aggressive: applies to all decimals, not just bounded-by-1 quantities).

Usage

f0(x, digits = 3, keep_sign = FALSE)

Arguments

x

Numeric vector (or anything coercible to numeric). NA passes through as NA_character_.

digits

Number of decimal places. Default 3.

keep_sign

Logical; if TRUE, prepend an explicit "+" for non-negative non-zero values. Default FALSE.

Value

A character vector the same length as x.

Examples

f0(c(0.123, -0.045, 1.234, NA))         # ".123", "-.045", "1.234", NA
#> [1] ".123"  "-.045" "1.234" NA     
f0(c(0.1, -0.001), digits = 2)          # ".10", "-.00"
#> [1] ".10"  "-.00"
f0(0.5, keep_sign = TRUE)               # "+.500"
#> [1] "+.500"
